key_elements.order: created_at uses DESC for efficient range queries

patterns_discovered: Alembic migration for indexes can use `sa.Index(...).create(op.get_bind(), checkfirst=False)` pattern instead of `op.create_index()` for more control
Descending index on `created_at DESC` optimizes time-range queries common in chat history
Use `IF EXISTS` in downgrade SQL to make downgrade idempotent and safe for re-runs
Inline `sa.text()` wrapper for column names in Index definition
gotchas_discovered: First attempt used `op.create_index()` but was fixed to use `sa.Index().create()` pattern
File missing trailing newline at end of file
Index column order matters: user_id, project_id (equality filters) should come before created_at (range filter) for optimal query performance
The `created_at DESC` syntax requires careful handling in Index definition

approach_outcome.solution_type: alembic migration with SQLAlchemy Index object
approach_outcome.optimization_applied: composite index with descending order on timestamp for efficient date-range queries

recommendations: Consider adding `checkfirst=True` if index may already exist in some deployments
Add trailing newline to migration file for POSIX compliance
Document index column order rationale in migration comments for future maintainers
Consider adding `_timestamp` pattern to migration filename for better versioning if not using UUID-style timestamp
